spec_match.rationale: The lab now exercises the relevant transition-property branches: custom retiming, transition: initial, transition: none, and a 0ms duration switch while leaving the active transition running.

interactive.rationale: Duration, easing, switch timing, switch mode, and replay controls all drive live boxes and telemetry after the broken escaped script was repaired.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still demonstrates that running transitions keep their starting snapshot while only future transitions use the new values.

edge_cases.rationale: The controls expose the exact edge cases from the motivation: switching to initial, none, or zero duration mid-flight versus the pre-139 cancelled or re-timed behavior.

references.rationale: The see-also section now cites https://chromestatus.com/feature/5194501932711936 and the CSS Transitions starting section.

design_system.rationale: The page keeps the local controls on shared design variables, with button background and text color paired.

summary: Transition Property Lab now runs valid JavaScript, links the correct ChromeStatus entry, and demonstrates the initial, none, zero-duration, and custom transition-property switch branches that motivated the Chrome 139 fix.
